data.content:
### Recharts Public API Import Example (TypeScript) Source: https://github.com/recharts/recharts/blob/main/DEVELOPING.md Illustrates the correct method for importing components and types from the Recharts library, using its designated public API entry point. This adheres to the project's import restrictions, ensuring stability and preventing linter failures. ```typescript import { TooltipIndex, DataKey, BarRectangleItem } from 'recharts'; ``` -------------------------------- ### Import Pie Component and Stories from Recharts Source: https://github.com/recharts/recharts/blob/main/storybook/stories/API/polar/Pie.mdx Imports the Pie component stories and Storybook documentation blocks for rendering interactive component documentation. Uses Storybook's Meta, Canvas, and Controls components to display the Pie component API and interactive controls. ```javascript import { Meta, Canvas, Controls } from '@storybook/addon-docs/blocks'; import * as ComponentStories from './Pie.stories'; ``` -------------------------------- ### Using Custom Recharts Components with BarChart (Recharts v3.0) Source: https://github.com/recharts/recharts/wiki/3.0-migration-guide This example demonstrates how to integrate custom React components, such as `MyCustomAxes`, directly within a Recharts v3.0 chart. In previous versions (v2.0), this direct embedding was not supported, but v3.0 allows for greater flexibility by treating custom components as first-class citizens. This approach simplifies chart customization and component reuse. ```jsx const MyCustomAxes = () => ( ) ``` -------------------------------- ### Generate Recharts API Documentation for Components Source: https://github.com/recharts/recharts/blob/main/omnidoc/README.md Utilize the API Documentation Generator to auto-generate API documentation files from TypeScript source code. This tool reads comments and type definitions, overwriting existing `en-US` files in `www/src/docs/api`. You can specify individual components as arguments or generate for all allowlisted components if no arguments are provided. ```shell npm run omnidoc [component1] [component2] ... ``` ```shell npm run omnidoc ``` ```shell npm run omnidoc CartesianGrid ``` ```shell npm run omnidoc XAxis YAxis ZAxis ``` -------------------------------- ### Render PieChart Canvas in Storybook Source: https://github.com/recharts/recharts/blob/main/storybook/stories/API/chart/PieChart.mdx Sets up the Storybook Meta component for PieChart documentation and renders the Simple story example in a Canvas with padded layout. This creates an interactive preview of the PieChart component. ```jsx ``` -------------------------------- ### Directly Embedding Custom Component in Recharts v3.0 Source: https://github.com/recharts/recharts/wiki/3.0-migration-guide This example highlights the new flexibility in Recharts v3.0, allowing direct embedding of `MyCustomComponent` within a `BarChart`. This approach renders the custom component directly, eliminating the need for the `Customized` wrapper. It simplifies the component structure and improves readability for custom chart elements. ```jsx ``` -------------------------------- ### Display Pie Component Storybook Documentation Source: https://github.com/recharts/recharts/blob/main/storybook/stories/API/polar/Pie.mdx Sets up Storybook documentation for the Pie component using Meta, Canvas, and Controls. The Canvas displays the API story with padded layout, and Controls renders interactive property controls for the component. ```jsx ``` -------------------------------- ### Create a Recharts LineChart Component in React Source: https://github.com/recharts/recharts/blob/main/README.md This example demonstrates how to compose a basic LineChart using Recharts React components. It includes an XAxis, Tooltip, CartesianGrid, and multiple Line elements to display data. ```jsx ``` -------------------------------- ### Import Trapezoid with Storybook Documentation Setup Source: https://github.com/recharts/recharts/blob/main/storybook/stories/API/shapes/Trapezoid.mdx Imports necessary Storybook addon modules and Trapezoid component stories for documentation and interactive testing. This setup enables the Meta, Canvas, and Controls components to display component API documentation and interactive controls in Storybook. ```javascript import { Meta, Canvas, Controls } from '@storybook/addon-docs/blocks'; import * as ComponentStories from './Trapezoid.stories'; ``` -------------------------------- ### Import XAxis Component and Stories in Storybook Source: https://github.com/recharts/recharts/blob/main/storybook/stories/API/cartesian/XAxis.mdx Imports the Meta, Canvas, and Controls utilities from Storybook addon-docs for documentation, along with XAxis component stories. This setup enables interactive documentation and API control panels in Storybook. ```javascript import { Meta, Canvas, Controls } from '@storybook/addon-docs/blocks'; import * as ComponentStories from './XAxis.stories'; ``` -------------------------------- ### Display Component Properties with ArgTypes Source: https://github.com/recharts/recharts/blob/main/storybook/stories/API/chart/RadarChart.mdx Renders an interactive ArgTypes table displaying all configurable properties of the RadarChart component, sorted with required properties first. This allows developers to explore and understand the component's API directly in the documentation. ```JavaScript ``` -------------------------------- ### Import PieChart and Storybook Components Source: https://github.com/recharts/recharts/blob/main/storybook/stories/API/chart/PieChart.mdx Imports necessary Storybook documentation components and PieChart story definitions for rendering interactive documentation. Demonstrates the typical setup for documenting Recharts components using Storybook's addon-docs. ```javascript import { Canvas, Meta, ArgTypes } from '@storybook/addon-docs/blocks'; import * as PieChartStories from './PieChart.stories'; ``` -------------------------------- ### ComposedChart Component Structure Source: https://github.com/recharts/recharts/blob/main/storybook/stories/API/chart/ComposedChart.mdx The ComposedChart component is a container for composed chart visualizations that can display multiple data series with different chart types simultaneously. It requires a ResponsiveContainer parent and supports multiple child components for customization. ```APIDOC ## ComposedChart Component ### Description A flexible charting component that combines multiple chart types (Area, Line, Bar) in a single visualization. Used for displaying complex data with multiple dimensions and series. ### Component Hierarchy #### Parent Component - **ResponsiveContainer** - Required wrapper component that provides responsive sizing #### Child Components (Supported) - **XAxis** - Horizontal axis component - **YAxis** - Vertical axis component - **CartesianGrid** - Grid lines for better readability - **Tooltip** - Interactive data point information display - **Legend** - Chart legend for data series identification - **Area** - Area chart data series - **Line** - Line chart data series - **Bar** - Bar chart data series - **ReferenceArea** - Highlighted area reference zone - **ReferenceLine** - Horizontal or vertical reference line - **ReferenceDot** - Reference point marker - **Brush** - Data range selector component - **Customized** - Custom shape components - **Valid SVG Elements** - Additional SVG elements for custom rendering ### Properties Refer to ArgTypes documentation for complete property listing. Note: Properties in the 'Other' and 'Internal' groups are not recommended for use. ### Basic Usage Example ```jsx import { ComposedChart, Area, Line, Bar, XAxis, YAxis, CartesianGrid, Tooltip, Legend, ResponsiveContainer } from 'recharts'; const data = [ { name: 'Jan', uv: 4000, pv: 2400, amt: 2400 }, { name: 'Feb', uv: 3000, pv: 1398, amt: 2210 } ]; ``` ``` -------------------------------- ### Render Interactive Canvas with Simple RadarChart Story Source: https://github.com/recharts/recharts/blob/main/storybook/stories/API/chart/RadarChart.mdx Displays an interactive Canvas component showing the Simple RadarChart story with padded layout. This provides a visual example of the RadarChart component in action within the Storybook documentation. ```JavaScript ``` -------------------------------- ### Cell Component Usage in Recharts Source: https://github.com/recharts/recharts/blob/main/storybook/stories/API/component/Cell.mdx Cell wrapper component specification for use within Pie, Bar, RadialBar, Scatter, or Funnel components. Cell allows specification of individual child attributes with props that have higher priority than parent component data attributes. ```jsx // Cell can be wrapped by Pie, Bar, or RadialBar // In Pie, for example, specify attributes of each child node through Cell props // Cell props have higher priority than parent component data attributes ``` -------------------------------- ### Implementing Recharts Tooltip with Portal (Recharts v3.0) Source: https://github.com/recharts/recharts/wiki/3.0-migration-guide This example demonstrates how to use the new `portal` prop on the `Tooltip` component in Recharts v3.0. By providing a `portalRef`, developers can render the tooltip's DOM element into a specific external container. This allows for fine-grained control over the tooltip's positioning, styling, and z-index outside of the SVG context, addressing common layout challenges. ```jsx const TooltipWithPortal = () => { const [portalRef, setPortalRef] = useState(null); return ( <> {portalRef && }
{ if (portalRef == null && node != null) { setPortalRef(node); } }} >

Inspect the resulting HTML to see that the Tooltip element is rendered according to the portal ref.

You now control the container the Tooltip renders in, as well as style attributes such as width, height, etc. Those can be managed via the Tooltip wrapperStyle prop.

{/* The Tooltip will render here */}
); }; ``` -------------------------------- ### Import Storybook Documentation and Stories (JavaScript/MDX) Source: https://github.com/recharts/recharts/blob/main/storybook/stories/API/chart/ScatterChart.mdx This code imports essential components for rendering documentation within Storybook's MDX format, including Canvas, Meta, and ArgTypes. It also imports all defined stories from './ScatterChart.stories' to power the interactive examples and property tables. ```javascript import { Canvas, Meta, ArgTypes } from '@storybook/addon-docs/blocks'; import * as ScatterChartStories from './ScatterChart.stories'; ```
